description_long: This object is a shoulder-fired anti-tank weapon, visually resembling the RPG-7, with a distinctive long cylindrical barrel and a wide conical muzzle at the front. The main body is composed of a wood-textured midsection and grip, contrasting with the matte black metal components at the front and rear. The weapon features two prominent vertical grips, one near the trigger and another forward, both with a wood finish. The rear section is slightly tapered and ends in a black circular buttplate, while the front section flares out into a large, black, bell-shaped muzzle. Iron sights are mounted on the top, with the rear sight near the shooter and the front sight at the end of the barrel. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible screws and mechanical details, suggesting robust construction for field use.
description: A shoulder-fired anti-tank weapon with a wood-textured body, black metal barrel, and a wide conical muzzle, featuring iron sights and dual vertical grips.
description_view_0: The weapon is seen from the rear, showing the circular black buttplate and the wood-textured body extending forward.
description_view_1: A full side profile reveals the long cylindrical barrel, wood-textured grips, iron sights, and the wide conical muzzle at the front.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the large, black, bell-shaped muzzle and the alignment of the barrel and sights.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile mirrors view 1, showing the wood and metal construction, dual grips, and the conical muzzle.
